title: JWT auth dependency — protect API endpoints
description: Create a reusable FastAPI dependency that extracts and validates the JWT from the Authorization header, returning the current authenticated user. All protected endpoints will use this dependency.
acceptanceCriteria: get_current_user dependency extracts Bearer token from Authorization header
Valid token returns the User object from the database
Token pointing to a deleted user returns 401
Protected endpoints return 401 when called without a token

steps.details: Create src/api/dependencies/auth.py. Import HTTPBearer and HTTPAuthorizationCredentials from fastapi.security, HTTPException, Depends, db dependency, User model, and decode_access_token. Define security = HTTPBearer(). Implement get_current_user(credentials: HTTPAuthorizationCredentials = Depends(security), db: Session = Depends(get_db)) -> User: call decode_access_token(credentials.credentials), extract user_id from payload['sub'], query db for User with that id, if not found raise HTTPException(401, 'User not found'), return the user.
In src/api/routers/auth.py, add GET /me endpoint with Depends(get_current_user). Return UserResponse from the current user. This endpoint verifies the full auth flow works end-to-end.

Login to get a token, then: GET http://localhost:8000/api/auth/me with Authorization: Bearer <token> — should return user object. GET same endpoint with no Authorization header — should return 401. GET with 'Bearer invalidtoken' — should return 401.

technicalNotes: Use HTTPBearer() (not OAuth2PasswordBearer) for simpler Bearer token extraction
Wrap decode_access_token in try/except JWTError and raise HTTPException(401) on failure
The 'sub' claim in the JWT payload contains the user's string UUID id
